commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer comfort with the generated-state compatibility risk; there is no narrow code repair finding for automation.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the diff; it narrows a false-positive SecretRef audit path without adding dependencies, workflow execution, or broader secret exposure. (sha=6954f219d518e7d89df8a4df3c7ab4423dbc80db)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes generated model provider auth markers to `secretref-env:<NAME>`, resolves those markers at model-registry request time, and exempts them from secrets-audit plaintext findings.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +127. Total +146 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current source returns env-backed auth-profile `keyRef` values as bare env names and the audit flags unknown string apiKey values as plaintext; I did not run the CLI in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer comfort with the generated-state compatibility risk; there is no narrow code repair finding for automation.

target.title: fix(models): keep generated secret refs out of plaintext
target.body:
Fixes #88562.

## Summary

- write generated env SecretRef-backed provider `apiKey` values as `secretref-env:<NAME>` markers instead of bare env-var names
- teach generated model auth resolution to resolve those markers from the process environment
- keep `openclaw security audit --deep` from treating generated env SecretRef markers as plaintext credentials

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Per-agent `models.json` generation could persist auth-profile env `keyRef` values as bare strings such as `TENSORIX_API_KEY`, which looked like plaintext to the deep security audit and produced false-positive `PLAINTEXT_FOUND` findings.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/andy/openclaw-88562`, branch `fix/models-json-secret-ref-88562`, commit `6954f219d5`.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.auth-aliases.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.auth-provenance.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.cloudflare-ai-gateway.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.vercel-ai-gateway.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts src/secrets/audit.test.ts
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
```

Evidence after fix: The focused generator, model-registry, and audit tests passed, and the outgoing commit author was verified:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  25 passed (25)
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  57 passed (57)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 17.78s
6954f219d5 Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> Fix models json secret ref api keys
```

Observed result after fix: A generated provider backed by an auth-profile env `keyRef` now gets `apiKey: "secretref-env:TENSORIX_API_KEY"`; `ModelRegistry` resolves that marker through `process.env.TENSORIX_API_KEY`, and the deep audit no longer reports `PLAINTEXT_FOUND` or unresolved SecretRef for that marker.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Tensorix provider request; runtime behavior is covered at the generated config and model registry credential-resolution boundary.
target.author: turbotheturtle

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
